primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with multi-organ dysfunction (lactate 4.2 mmol/L, WBC 14.2 K/uL, AKI Stage 3, coagulopathy)
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates persistent lactic acidosis (4.2 mmol/L), thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL), elevated bilirubin (1.4 mg/dL), and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL). AKI has progressed from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) within 48 hours. Leukocytosis and lactate elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Platelet count remains below 100 K/uL, meeting SOFA coagulation scoring thresholds.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI Stage 3 meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine rose from 2.1 to 8.9 mg/dL in 48 hours, exceeding KDIGO Stage 3 threshold
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring and indicates severe thrombocytopenia
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Perform source control investigation for infection
Administer fluid resuscitation per sepsis bundle
Monitor need for vasopressors and lactate clearance
Consider platelet transfusion if bleeding risk present
Evaluate for renal replacement therapy eligibility
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen integrity. Confirm infection source and assess response to initial resuscitation. Monitor platelet trends and AKI progression.

disease_progression.observation: AKI progressed from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9) in 48 hours
Platelets declined from 95 to 88 K/uL despite no evidence of bleeding

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
